what_it_does: Defines ignored filesystem paths and file patterns for repository-local generated, dependency, source-drop, render, debug-log, and Python bytecode artifacts.
observations: Ignores source/ and reference/ with a comment describing them as on-disk material piles rather than repo content.
Ignores renders/_* as session working shots.
standouts: Separates source drops from tracked repo content using comments.
Includes both frontend dependency noise and Python bytecode noise.
Contains a repeated ignore rule for mcp_registration_debug.log.
conventions: Uses standard .gitignore directory patterns with trailing slashes.
Groups entries with short explanatory comments.
Uses glob-style ignore syntax for renders/_* and *.pyc.
Breaks tidiness by repeating the same debug-log pattern.
notes: This file keeps dependency folders, local material drops, render working shots, debug logs, and Python bytecode out of version control.
questions: Are source/ and reference/ always meant to remain untracked local drops?
Should renders/_* ignore only temporary shots while allowing other render assets to be tracked?
purpose_vs_actual: The comments describe local materials, session shots, tool noise, and bytecode exclusions, and the ignore patterns match those stated purposes.
apparent_themes: separation of working materials from repository content
mixed JavaScript and Python tooling awareness
intention_signals: It appears intended to keep the repository focused on authored project content while excluding dependencies, local source/reference drops, temporary render outputs, debug logs, and bytecode artifacts.
novelty: The source/ and reference/ directories are explicitly framed as material piles rather than repository content.
contribution: Contributes a file-local artifact boundary that preserves authored content while excluding dependency installs, local material drops, temporary renders, tool logs, and bytecode caches from a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A .gitignore file for counterpart-design that excludes node_modules, local source/reference drops, render working shots, MCP debug logs, and Python bytecode artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: This was for keeping local materials, generated outputs, dependency folders, tool noise, and bytecode caches out of version control.
